title: Partial Order
statement:
A relation $\leq$ on a set $S$ is a \emph{partial order} if it satisfies,
for all $a, b, c \in S$:
- \textbf{Reflexivity:} $a \leq a$.
- \textbf{Anti-symmetry:} $a \leq b \land b \leq a \Longrightarrow a = b$.
- \textbf{Transitivity:} $a \leq b \land b \leq c \Longrightarrow a \leq c$.
The pair $(S, \leq)$ is called a \emph{partially ordered set}, or \emph{poset}.
